questionText: Which hydride among the following is less stable?
subject: Chemistry
solution:
The stability of hydrides depends on their bonding and the electronegativity difference between the elements. The greater the electronegativity difference, the more stable the hydride. Based on this, the order of stability of the given hydrides is:

HF > NH$$_3$$ > LiH > BeH$$_2$$

Therefore, the less stable hydride among the given options is BeH$$_2$$.

questionText:
Match List I with List II

LIST I
Type of Hydride		 LIST II
Example
A. Electron deficient hydride I. $$\mathrm{MgH_2}$$
B. Electron rich hydride II. $$\mathrm{HF}$$
C. Electron precise hydride III. $$\mathrm{B_2H_6}$$
D. Saline hydride IV. $$\mathrm{CH_4}$$

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
subject: Chemistry
solution:
A Electron deficient hydride -> III $\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6$

B Electron rich hydride -> II $\mathrm{HF}$

C Electron precise hydride -> IV $\mathrm{CH}_4$

D Saline hydride -> I $\mathrm{MgH}_2$

questionText: If the boiling point of H<sub>2</sub>O is 373 K, the boiling point of H<sub>2</sub>S will be :
options: greater than 300 K but less than 373 K
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Boiling point of H2S < Boiling point of H2O

Boiling point of H2S = 213 K

questionText:
The correct statements among (a) to (d) are :
(a) saline hydrides produce H2 gas when reacted with H2O.
(b) reaction of LiAlH4 with BF3 leads to B2H6.
(c) PH3 and CH4 are electron - rich and electron - precise hydrides, respectively.
(d) HF and CH4 are called as molecular
subject: Chemistry
solution:
(a) NaH + H2O $$ \to $$ NaOH + H2

(b) 3LiAlH4 + 4BF3 $$ \to $$ 2B2H6 + 3LiF + 3AlF3

(c) PH3 is electron rich due to one lone pair while CH4 is electron precise hydride.

(d) HF and CH4 are molecular hydrides.

subject: Chemistry
solution: NaH is an example of ionic hydride which is also known as saline hydride.
